content: The human brain's active reconsolidation mechanism, where retrieved memories are dynamically updated instead of stored as fixed snapshots, can be adapted to build next-generation active learning agent memories that proactively revise outdated stored episodes during retrieval for better planning. This would replace the common static episodic memory storage paradigm in current 2024-2025 SOTA curious reinforcement learning agents.
source_topics: memory as active learning human brain mechanisms
trigger_topic: memory as active learning human brain mechanisms
